signals.headline: Trump says US will hit Iran 'hard' again today
signals.summary: President Donald Trump has said the US will hit Iran "hard" on Wednesday, after the two sides exchanged strikes overnight.
keyTakeaways: "We hit them hard yesterday and we're going to hit them hard again today," he said, speaking to reporters in the Oval Office.
US Central Command (Centcom) said on Wednesday night that forces had begun launching strikes at 17:15 Eastern Time (22:15 BST) against "multiple targets" in Iran.
It added in a post on X that the strikes were "in response to Iran's unwarranted and continued aggression".
signals.whyItMatters: Iran's president Masoud Pezeshkian said in a post on X following Trump's briefing that Iran "will stand firm against any pressure or threat".

signals.headline: In the Ukraine war, new ground-based drones are playing a key role on the battlefield
signals.summary: Ukraine is increasingly using ground-based drones to move supplies, evacuate wounded soldiers, and take on frontline tasks as both sides adapt to a battlefield shaped by robotics.
keyTakeaways: Ukraine is deploying land drones for logistics, reconnaissance, and dangerous frontline support roles.
The systems can reduce risk to soldiers while helping units operate under pressure near the front.
The shift shows how the war is accelerating a broader move toward robotic warfare.
signals.whyItMatters: The war in Ukraine continues to reshape modern combat, turning low-cost robotics into a central part of battlefield strategy.

signals.headline: Police use water cannon against rioters in Northern Ireland
signals.summary: Police have used water cannon against rioters in Northern Ireland during a second night of anti-immigration protests.
keyTakeaways: “We have witnessed a lot of false information circulating on social media which is now forcing us to clarify that our loved one is in fact in a stable condition, and we are solely focused on his recovery at this time,” the family said in a statement issued via police.
Police tried to extinguish the flames.
One police officer was also struck on the head, but not believed to be seriously injured.
signals.whyItMatters: Ministers in the Stormont executive issued a joint statement condemning Tuesday night’s disorder, saying it had spread fear and risked innocent lives.

signals.headline: Lawsuit challenges Trump administration's land swap with SpaceX in Texas
signals.summary: Environmental groups on Wednesday sued in an attempt to stop the Trump administration from giving SpaceX more than 700 acres (280 hectares) of wildlife refuge in Texas , claiming it would worsen ecological risks to a Gulf coast region already transformed by billionaire Elon Musk ’s rocket operations.
keyTakeaways: The 103,000-acre (41,700-hectare) refuge spans four counties along the Texas border and is home to animal habitats and historical landmarks.
Maps show the land SpaceX would acquire would be closer to the company’s launchpad near the US-Mexico border.

The exchange would be the first time the US government has swapped land in the area with SpaceX, said Laiken Jordahl, a spokesperson with the Center for Biological Diversity, which filed the lawsuit alongside other opponents of the deal.
signals.whyItMatters: The US Fish and Wildlife Service this month approved moving forward with the deal with SpaceX, which would surrender 683 acres (276 hectares) the company owns in exchange for federal land in the Lower Rio Grande Valley national wildlife refuge.

signals.headline: A conservative California county is trying to kill mail-in voting
signals.summary: Shasta County, California, has backed a measure that would sharply limit mail-in voting and require most elections to happen in person on a single day.
keyTakeaways: Measure B would restrict absentee voting, require photo ID, and call for hand-counted ballots.
Voting-rights groups say the proposal conflicts with California law and could make voting harder for residents.
The county is likely headed for another legal clash with the state over election rules.
signals.whyItMatters: The fight shows how local election rules remain a major front in the broader US debate over voting access and election trust.
